desc:
Slanting sharply the cliff wall rises towards the sky, gaining height as it
travels southward. Further south a large plateau opens up atop the sheer rock
wall. Below runs a small trail that curves and continues just to the north;
beyond the trail everything is green as the vast plains stretch towards the
horizon.
name: Rising Cliff Wall

desc:
This small cracked trail is losing the battle against the malignant grasses that
grow all over the surrounding plains. Following the rising cliff wall that
reaches towards the sky both to the east and south, the trail runs north and
west from this point onwards. The high wall of rock seems to rise sharply as it
travels southward although at this point it would still be possible to climb.
name: Old Path

desc:
The small path curves here, following the towering cliff wall that rises high in
the sky to your east. The ground is cracked and hard and the once well-used path
is being reclaimed by the sturdy grasses of the eternally expanding plains that
surround it.
name: Faint Path

desc:
Small paths that lie to the east and south border these grassy plains. Though
growing wildly the grass here is clearly trampled regularly by some heavy
beasts; the tracks look as if they were made by a creature with large paws. Half
buried bones from some old carcasses give a hint that there might be large
predators around.
name: The Rolling Plains

desc:
Bordered to the south by a small path these plains offer no hiding place for
even small animals. The denser vegetation to the east gives the animal life some
cover, so the area is not totally devoid of fauna. The small spring to the north
provides ample water for the animals and plant life.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The grass grows thick and high as the fallow and lush ground is once again
reclaimed from cultivation and covered by the sturdy plants. The rustle of
swaying blades of grass blend with the babbling of a small stream just north of
here, creating a sharp thin line in the landscape, stretching out in all
directions.
name: An Overgrown Pasture

desc:
Just south of the swiftly flowing stream lies this abandoned landing. A small
ruined wooden pier runs a few yards out into the water. Further out single
wooden poles can be seen as the sole remains of a once well-constructed
structure. All around there are high sturdy grasses, diligently working to
reclaim what wild lands were once theirs as they cover the land in a dense and
green blanket.
name: Abandoned Landing

desc:
The banks of the rapidly flowing river, just to the north, used to be fallow and
rich pasture lands, crowded with domesticated beasts that spent their days
grazing in the grass. The babbling of the water mixes with the rustling of the
high grass, moving slowly in the wind, to create a soft background noise for
this setting. To the east lies an old landing, murky and abandoned for ages, and
a neglected, now overgrown, field lies dormant to the south.
name: Overgrown Pastures

desc:
A swift flowing river runs along a deep cutting several feet below. The ground
drops steeply in an uneven wall of rock dampened by the mists that hang over the
water. Brambles and briars fill any tiny gap among the stones covered in thick,
green moss. Below, the waters flow swiftly, singing loudly as they pass by the
rock faces.
name: Stony Riverside

desc:
This large plateau opens up and slowly climbs higher to the south and west and
slants down to the east, where it soon reaches a once great road that now lies
crumbled and ruined. The ground is still covered in grass but the turf grows
sparser here, as the soil is not as fertile atop the plateau.
name: Large Plateau

desc:
This entire plateau slopes towards the east, as if reaching out for the old road
running at its feet. Still covered by sparse grasses, the ground is rather hard
and the rock shines through in several spots. The plants that grow here seem to
live their lives in constant battle against the strong winds that occasionally
threaten their existence.
name: Grassy Plateau

desc:
In a small depression on this cliff a few vultures have built their nest,
allowing a clear and undisturbed vantage point for the chicks when they hatch.
The nest is made of several large branches and huge piles of twigs thrust into a
large circular design, carefully sheltering the inhabitants from the worst of
the howling winds.
name: Cliff's Edge

desc:
The plateau levels out here as it reaches the cliff wall's edge. The rock lies
bare as the soil has been swept away over the decades by the fierce winds that
howl by. A few small tufts of grass have managed to get a grip in a small crack
but they are dry and ragged, fighting a losing battle against the rough elements
of nature.
name: The Windy Platform

desc:
This small path was once well used but now it is cracked and abandoned. The wild
grasses are closing in and slowly devouring it as the years go by. The remains
of the path run west and north from this point, curving to avoid hitting the
cliff wall rising to the east.
name: Abandoned Path

desc:
Looking high up into the sky, one notices this place is below a sheer cliff
wall. The grass does not grow well here, as the ground lies in shadow most of
the day. The rock wall rising above has been polished by the rain and wind
making any ascent impossible. Just north of here is a faint old path trudging
along through the vast plains. To the west there are some abandoned fields.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Below the branches and twigs in the nest there are several cracks in the stone.
Small piles of crushed leaves have fallen down here, as well as a large number
of tiny bones from various small animals. Piles of rubbish and dirt cover almost
everything and a few small loose stones lie scattered in the dust.
name: Below the Nest

desc:
Standing just beside a faint and overgrown path lie these remnants of what seems
to have been a large wooden structure. The building is ruined and cracked, the
roof has fallen in and the walls are on the verge of collapse. Parts of the
building have been blackened by fire and large portions of the walls have rotted
away. All around the old house lie the grassy plains, stretching out towards the
distant horizon.
name: A Rotting Old Structure

desc:
The path curves here, gradually changing direction to lead both north and east.
A tiny branch of the path continues westward, almost buried below the thick and
sturdy grasses. Lying all around are the endless expanses of grass-covered
fields that have been reclaimed by nature as the years passed by.
name: Curving Path

desc:
An old and almost completely covered path leads travellers through the high
grasses here, towards the remnants of an old farming village just to the west.
The path turns here, continuing on north and east, through the old and abandoned
fields that surround it.
name: Tiny Path

desc:
What was once a house of some sort is now merely a pile of rotting and decaying
wood topped with a slanting roof. The doorpost still stands and the broken door
hangs loosely on rusted hinges, seemingly ready to collapse at the slightest
disturbance. All the walls have caved in and the entire house seems unstable and
ready to crash down at any moment.
name: Abandoned Farm

desc:
Once an old corral for bred horses, the fences are now rotting and murky. A
large heap of bones and skulls lies in the far southwest corner and a foul
stench of death and decay surrounds you. The once so lush and green grass is
withered and dead and there are faint imprints made by large paws in the soft
soil.
name: An Old Corral

desc:
Overgrown by dark reeds and vines the old fields are now hostile and dark. Any
trace of the old farmers have since long been destroyed and now only vile nature
leaves its trademark on the forgotten field. Imprinted in the soft soil are the
faint marks of great paws.
name: Overgrown Fields

desc:
This old field, once used by farmers inhabiting the surrounding area, is now
overgrown and covered in tall and sturdy weeds that have reclaimed the land. The
soft soil below shows markings of recent passage, as parts of the grass are
trampled and bent. Surrounding the field are several ancient ruined buildings,
some of which are merely wooden skeletons, blackened and cracked. North of here
lies abandoned pasture land. The overgrown fields reach out further to the west.
name: An Old Field

desc:
A small path leads through the grass, passing a crumbled ruin as it turns, to
the southwest. The path is almost completely lost to the fierce grasses that
fight to reclaim the land where the trail was marked. The ruined wooden
structure is an old farm, burned and cracked, leaving only a wooden skeleton as
a reminder of the once proud village. North lies an old and abandoned landing
just visible from this place, while to the southwest are some old abandoned
fields.
name: Crumbled Ruins

desc:
This faint path stretches out as a thin, vague line through the grass covered
old fields reaching from east to west. All around lie overgrown and abandoned
pastures and fields that are now being reclaimed and restored to their
pre-cultivation status by the fiercely growing grasses. Further west the
remnants of a small farming settlement can be seen.
name: A Faint Path

desc:
Running east to west this old path lies abandoned and broken. What was once a
broad and well-travelled trail has now become narrow and partly covered in grass
as the hardy grasses slowly, but inevitably, reclaim this once populated land.
Stretching out northward lies a vast expanse of plains, resembling a large green
ocean. South of here there lies an old rotting building. The path continues both
to the east and west, valiantly fighting a losing battle against the encroaching
grass.
name: Abandoned Path

desc:
Coming from the west and leading off to the east, this path has clearly not been
in regular use for ages. The plains to the north and the old fields to the south
slowly creep towards the path with the clear intent of overwhelming it. On the
portions of the small path that remain faint tracks of large paws can be seen.
name: A Faint Path

desc:
Once someone lived and farmed this land, but now the fields lie long forgotten
and long grass has been given free reign. To the west you can distinguish some
kind of old structure where whomever farmed the land might have lived. East and
south vast, lifeless plains stretch out and to the north you can spot a small
path zig-zaging across the fields.
name: Overgrown Fields

desc:
This old structure was once a barn, that much is obvious from the remnants of
the building. It seems to have been partly attacked by flames at some time as
part of the wall and ceiling to your west is blackened and cracked. The northern
part of the house has caved in, allowing the wind and rain to freely ravage the
old barn. Several of the walls are rotting and the overall impression is one of
decay and age.
name: An Old Barn

desc:
The grass here is withered and yellow, almost as if life was sucked out from it.
You notice that there is a clear difference as you look at the grass from north
to the south, changing from yellow to a bleak green. At the horizon to your
north you see some kind of stones lined up, almost as an army, marching to war.
name: The Withered Grass

desc:
The plains here are dead. Nothing grows or lives here. No sound can be heard and
no animal can be seen. The plains are as green as ever south and west of you,
but north and east they seem withered and dying. Northeast of you there seem to
be some black shapes at the horizon.
name: The Dead Grass Plains

desc:
The ground is irregular here, broken by a few small cracks and a few low hills.
A small rabbit hole can be seen in one of the small hills, and sounds of
movement can be heard in the grass near it. A few yards from the rabbit hole
lies a large flat rock on which snakes seem to sunbathe, as indicated by the
remains of a scaly reptile skin on the rock's surface.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
In the high surrounding grass, you can see small cracks in the ground. As you
look closer, you see that they get wider as they continue north, opening up into
a wide, deep canyon. The passage north thus being effectively blocked, the only
route available seems to be along the grass-covered plains to your east and
west. Not much else can be seen in the flowing greenery, but small animals
create a veil of background noise, embedding you in the everyday sounds of
nature.
name: The Cracked Plains

desc:
In the plains here a few small bushes grow in the short, thick grass. The bushes
are not very high or thick, but they seem to compete with the grasses for the
space to live. So far, the bushes seem to be on the loosing side of the war.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The wind makes a soft rustle in the high grass, making it flow back and forth,
gently rocking in the breeze. The ground you pass on is soft and gives a little
as you travel along. First, it almost seems as if you are on an endless plain
that stretches out eternally, but finally you spot a small black dot on the
northern horizon. Judging by the distance, the dot must be very large to be seen
from here.
name: Flowing Plains

desc:
Close to the old broken road, the plains are still covered in thick and high
grass, rising as a mighty wave that is rolling in towards the road, to crush it
and sweep away the last traces of humanity. Still the road runs in a straight
line in the green of the plains, a clearly visible landmark.
name: The Wild Plains

desc:
The grassy plain here is bustling with activity, small animals rustling about in
the undergrowth chasing insects, and birds singing high in the sky. Not even
your presence or the proximity of the old abandoned road seem to dampen the
sounds of living nature.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
Here the plains have exploded into life. The nearby water has made it possible
for the grasses to grow tall and thick, making the passage difficult. As the
plants thrive, so do the animals. Everywhere you look you see movement and hear
the sounds of animals running about in the grass. All around you the plains
stretch out, with a few exceptions. Just southeast of here you spot a small
thicket of trees and due west you see the faint outlines of a road running as a
silver line in the plains.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
Absolutely nothing grows here. The ground lies bare, the soil long since blown
away since nothing could bind it here. Somehow the death and decay of the area
seem to emanate from the centre of the large stones that stand on top of a small
burial mound. The stones are black and about two yards high. There are four of
them, standing in a circle, with a smaller, flat stone block lying in the
middle. Not even the wind seems to blow there, and everything seems to slow
down, waiting for something to happen.
name: An Ancient Burial Mound

desc:
The area seems silent, almost dead, but you can feel the small animals move
around you and small insects fly by. Still nothing can be heard. The farther
east you look, the lower and bleaker the grasses appear. To the west the land is
as green as ever, but north and south it does not seem any better.
name: Silent Plains

desc:
West of you the ground opens up into a deep and dark chasm. The ground must have
been cracked and broken by something powerful to split up in such a way. The
canyon stretches out further south, trailing off in the distant southwest. Even
though the ground is cracked and broken, the grasses still thrive here. They
even grow a bit down the canyon walls, as if trying to claim the cleft for
themselves.
name: Near the Chasm

desc:
As you continue your travel along the grassy plains, you notice that the smooth
ground has been broken here. As the ground split open, a winding chasm has
formed here, preventing any passage farther east or south. The chasm is not so
wide, but still broad enough to make it impossible to pass. The plants and
grasses that live here do not seem bothered though, but grow all the way out to
the edge, and even a bit down on the chasm's walls.
name: Plains by a Chasm

desc:
Just west of you is a small thicket of trees, which is the only noticeable
landmark for some distance. The ground here is soft, covered in low but sturdy
grasses that keep the soil in place. Far to your north you can barely discern a
vague outline of a small and thin needle-like construct and through a haze you
can just make out the far reaches of the Misty Mountains in the east.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
In the ground there is a small depression, that over the years has collected
water and thus has almost become a small lake. The vegetation is dense; several
types of grasses and small trees grow here. Just south of the depression some
trees have managed to grow taller, creating some shadowy places on these large
sunbathed plains.
name: Sunken Plains

desc:
Just north of you lie what seems to be remnants of an old field, and you can
spot something that might have been an old pond just west. All around you the
grass still spreads out, covering the ground completely. Farther to your north
you can spot what seems to be a tower, rising among a dark thicket of some kind
of bushes.
name: Plains Bordering an Old Field

desc:
Surrounded by green, you find yourself in the middle of the flowing plains. Once
this might have been a field, where farmers worked for the old lord of the keep
that you see towering up over you to the north. The vast expanse of green
stretches out in all directions, only disturbed by a few small ruins in the
east. As you travel through the grass, you feel that the weeds are bubbling with
life as the small inhabitants of the earth run around, hidden from view, but
still creating a soft but perceivable wall of sound.
name: Plains Close to the Old Tower

desc:
It is hard to discern anything in the rubble of rock and rotting wood here, but
the mere size of the pile suggests that it should once have been a barn. The
proximity to the ruined farmhouse just to the north only strengthens the
impression. Now, the once cleared area is overgrown and wild, once again.
name: Ruins of a Barn

desc:
Here the plains seem absent-minded, as if they were waiting for something, or
someone. Nothing grows here, no animals pass here and the soil lies bare beneath
the sky. The surrounding plains do not look much better in the southeast, but as
you look north and west, they seem to be alive and thriving. A few small, dark
shapes can be spotted just at the south-eastern horizon.
name: Dead Plains

desc:
Even though the air is moist and the surrounding plains are lush and green, this
particular area seems dry and void of any animal life. The grasses growing here
are yellow and short, as if they are fighting for their very existence every
second of the day. As you scan the surroundings, you notice that no matter in
which direction you look, it only seems to get better to the north, and much
worse as you look south.
name: Dried-out Plains

desc:
Where once there must have been lush, dark green grass now there remains only
limp blades. A dull grey-green cast dominates the area and in places this is
being replaced by a yellow pall which increases in proportion the further to the
north you look. But if the grass is bad, the other vegetation is worse; the few
small bushes dotted across the area appear to be completely lifeless and even
the toughest weeds are hanging limp.
name: The Withered Grass

desc:
Further to the north it appears that the once verdant plains have been visited
by some sort of blight, for no vegetation now remains. The lush grass here,
however, did not succumb, growing as wild and untrammelled as ever. Amongst the
bright blades of grass a number of wildflowers and tough weeds also flourish,
but on such an exposed plain no larger plants seem able to survive particularly
well.
name: On the Open Plain

desc:
Long blades of grass dance upon the breeze, rippling and rolling like the water
of a lake lapping upon its shores. East and south the vegetation seems not
nearly so lush, but north and west of here the emerald ocean stretches far into
the distance.
name: A Lush Plain

desc:
Deep fissures scar the ground, running north to south across the landscape. To
the north they open out markedly, whilst to the south they disappear into a
field of massive boulders. A few stubborn weeds and thick tufts of yellow grass
grow between the cracks and boulders, and this vegetation becomes more lush even
as the clefts grow wider.
name: Broken Ground

desc:
Thin, rocky topsoil makes for only the poorest of vegetation in this area.
Further north the ground apparently improves and lush grasses dominate the
landscape, but here only the coarsest of weeds, a thin covering of sickly,
yellow grass and a small number of stunted bushes cling to life. East and south
the terrain worsens.
name: On a Bleak and Windswept Plain

desc:
Whilst to the north the plains boast lush vegetation the ground here is rather
rockier and its thin soil supports only tough clumps of yellow grass, coarse
weeds, and a few small and scattered bushes. With no protection from the
elements this is a bleak and uninviting area, perpetually buffeted by strong
winds.
name: On a Bleak and Windswept Plain

desc:
The vegetation here is spread rather thinly. Only a few stunted bushes, some
tough weeds, and some scattered tussocks of coarse, yellow grass have fought
their way through the rocky ground. A small circle of stones has been set on the
ground, not far from the eaves of the woods to the south, and a few small bones
lie on the ground beside it. To the east of here the landscape remains much the
same, but in all other directions the plant life grows more dominant.
name: Sparse Brush

desc:
A few stubborn bushes mark the outermost reaches of a forest which rises above
the landscape to the west of here. The predominant groundcover is loose stone
and small boulders, but a few tough hummocks of grass and some coarse weeds
sprout up amongst the rocks.
name: Scattered Brush

desc:
Deep fissures and small gullies criss-cross the landscape. Between these the
ground is covered by rocks, boulders and loose stone. Grass grows in thick
clumps wherever it can find purchase, but the soil seems to be thin and little
other vegetation can be seen in the area. To the west and north the landscape
seems more lush, but south and east ever more boulders and rather fewer plants
are to be seen.
name: Shattered Land

desc:
The land is split and scarred by dozens of narrow, parallel cracks which run
from north to south. Between them the ground is peppered by loose rocks and
stones, and no small number of massive boulders. To the north the fissures grow
wider; to the south it is the boulders which dominate the landscape.
name: Shattered Land

desc:
To the north the land is blanketed by a lush covering of grass, but here the
vegetation must do battle against the many rocks and stones that litter the
ground. The soil too is thin and coarse. Clumps of yellowing grass stand amid
the scattered rubble, but no larger plants can be seen in this area.
name: On a Rocky Plain

desc:
Plant life proliferates here. Thick, dark green grass grows tall and strong,
bent but not broken by the constant winds which assail the plains. Amid the lush
grass grow wildflowers which pepper the meadow with bright colour, and here and
there a small bush adds height to the otherwise flat landscape.
name: Lush Meadow

desc:
East, west and south of you the land is blanketed by rich vegetation. Here the
story is different; the grass is thin and yellowing, its limp blades bent and
broken by the wind. The small bushes which are scattered about the area appear
weak and diseased. To the north the picture steadily grows worse.
name: A Sickly Plain

desc:
A dense carpet of vegetation covers the ground. Tall grass and thick heather
battle for domination over the land. A number of small bushes and shrubs are
scattered amid the resulting patchwork of green and purple foliage, which
stretches far to the south. East and north the grass wins out, but westward the
plant life must contend with a harsher, more rocky landscape.
name: A Rugged Heath

desc:
Coarse tufts of grass and thick little weeds sprout up through the rocky ground.
A few small, hardy bushes have laid claim to the open areas between the larger
boulders which litter the landscape here. Westward the massive stones grow more
numerous and finding a clear route among them appears to be rather a difficult
task.
name: On a Rocky Expanse

desc:
All around you the land is covered by enormous boulders, as if an entire
hillside had been blasted apart. Some bear thin veins of metal or ore. Some are
rough, others almost perfectly smooth. Their size and variety, their uncountable
numbers, and their very presence here are a mute testimony to the grandeur and
to the dangers of Arda.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
An unforgiving wall of granite stands before you to the south, blocking the way
completely. Nor is there a clear route from this place to any other side, for
the entire area is crowded with gigantic boulders. Between them the ground is
covered by smaller rocks and stones, and of plant life you can see none.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
Gargantuan boulders clutter the landscape. Between them lie scattered rocks and
stones, and vegetation is almost completely absent. North and west there lie so
many of the massive stones that it is impossible to see any clear route. East
and south the way seems at least a little more open.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
A few rough bushes and shrubs have taken hold amongst the rocks and boulders as
the encroaching forest to the west slowly takes hold of this area. The uneven
ground makes treacherous footing, and the scattering of rocky debris becomes
more concentrated as you move to the east or south.
name: By the Border of a Twisted Forest

desc:
To the north and west of you a large, but strangely twisted, forest rises above
the landscape. A little of its tangled undergrowth seems to be spreading this
way, but it is hard for anything to grow in the thin soil of this area. Tumbled
piles of rocks and boulders are scattered about the place, and these grow more
numerous to the east. To the south the terrain begins a gentle climb into a
narrow ridge of hills.
name: By the Border of a Twisted Forest

desc:
Not far from the edge of the forest, and nestled against the flank of a range of
hills to the south, the land undergoes quite a dramatic change. Grass, brush and
trees are replaced by rocks, stones and a few huge boulders. The topsoil is
thin, almost completely absent in places, and even the most stubborn weeds seem
to have difficulty finding purchase in the rocky ground. To the east the terrain
actually worsens, with ever more boulders to be seen.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
To the south a solid wall of granite rears high above you. Massive boulders are
scattered across the land, as if an entire hillside somehow lay shattered here.
East and west the rocks become less numerous but to the north their numbers
increase, and it is hard to see any clear route between them.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
An unforgiving wall of granite stands before you to the south, blocking the way
completely. Nor is there a clear route from this place to any other side, for
the entire area is crowded with gigantic boulders. Between them the ground is
covered by smaller rocks and stones, and of plant life you can see none.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
The ground is entirely blanketed by rocks and stones. No appreciable amount of
vegetation can be seen here, with only a few weak stems of yellow grass peeping
up between the smallest pebbles. The landscape is dominated by vast numbers of
colossal boulders, interspersed by many monumental heaps of rather smaller
stones.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
The coarse grass and heather of the open moors is largely replaced here by a
jumble of rocks and stones. With the cold, hard pebbles and larger boulders
littering the landscape vegetation must fight for even a small foothold on the
area, and of larger plants there are almost none. Westward the rocks are even
more dominant and massive piles of stones, in addition to many massive boulders,
stand in your way.
name: Stony Meadow

desc:
A flat landscape stretches out before you in all directions. One could be
forgiven for thinking it featureless, but closer inspection shows that the
terrain is beautiful, albeit in a bleak and windswept fashion. Low tufts of
purple heather grow in profusion, offering bright patches of colour amid the
tough, yellow-green grass. In the far distance the Misty Mountains stand tall on
the horizon. Closer to here stand two lower ranges of hills, and not too far to
the south the land slopes away into a river valley.
name: Blustery Moorland

desc:
Climbing to the east, the land rises above the forest and plains into a narrow
ridge of hills. Though you are not upon a trail as such, few shrubs or large
plants obstruct your way and it seems comparatively easy to pick a route among
the rocks and boulders which lie scattered all about you.
name: A Rocky Path to the Hills

desc:
The land falls off precipitously to the north, making travel in that direction
quite impossible. To the west and south, however, it slopes gently toward the
plains below. The ground underfoot is a mass of rough stones and scree, making
your footing more than a little unsteady, and only a few small tufts of coarse,
yellow grass manage to grow in the thin topsoil.
name: Climbing along a Ridge

desc:
Though the slope down from the ridge to the south is rather steep it is as
nothing compared to the northern flank of this narrow ridgeline. It appears as
if at some past time the entire northern face of the hillside had been blasted
away, and the valley floor below is littered with gargantuan piles of rocks and
boulders.
name: Climbing along a Ridge

desc:
This ridge of hills stands high above the plains, completely exposed to the
elements. Westward a rough trail, made more by an absence of vegetation than by
any other agency, leads gently down; in all other directions the way is too
steep and littered by loose scree, making any attempt at descent impossible.
name: Climbing along a Ridge

desc:
A tall cliff rears above you to the west. Its cold, rocky face is broken by a
rough aperture which gapes wide, like the maw of some mighty beast. All around
you the ground is littered by massive boulders. No plant life has taken root
among them, the land instead being covered by smaller rocks and stones.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
Thick, purple heather and coarse tufts of yellow-green grass make a patchwork of
the land, but this colourful quilt is marred by the innumerable rocks and stones
which are littered across the entire area. Westward these grow larger and more
numerous, completely blocking the way. South the land slopes gently off toward a
river valley. Eastward stands a windswept expanse of open moors, and north the
stony meadow stretches out before you.
name: On a Rugged Heath

desc:
Only to the south does the land vary at all. In all other directions you see a
vast expanse of open moorland. Bleak in aspect, the terrain is dominated by
coarse tufts of low-growing grass, interspersed by bright patches of purple
heather. No larger plants seem able to thrive on this windswept ground. To the
south the land slopes gently downward, approaching a river valley.
name: Windswept Moors

desc:
The low northern shore of the river is comprised of soft, peaty earth and
appears to be somewhat marshy, judging by the vegetation. Though the current is
rather languid the waters nevertheless sweep along more dirt as they pass by.
Thus the river widens a little more to the north every day. To the south,
however, the bank is rocky and stands firm.
name: On a Lazy River

desc:
A firm, rocky bank holds the river to the south. The north shore is lower and
formed from soft, peaty soil. As the water swirls past, it picks up ever more of
the brown earth, carrying it west. The perpetual undercutting has made the river
rather wide and its waters flow lazily along. Further downstream, where the land
grows more rocky, the river is funnelled into a tighter course.
name: On a Lazy River

desc:
The river remains fairly wide here, and rather shallow. A number of small rocks
break its surface, making the waters dance and swirl about, sending a thin mist
into the air. Both shores are fairly low but rocky and strong, guiding the
waterway firmly along its westerly course.
name: Along a Rocky River

desc:
As the river progresses westwards its course becomes ever more narrow and ever
more rocky, causing it to splash and frolic, casting a fine mist into the air.
The banks here stand only a little higher than the water level, but they are
stony and firm. It will be many long years before these lively waters manage to
undercut the shore.
name: On a Rocky River

desc:
A small river traces its course through this sloping valley. Following the
waterway back towards its source, to the east, the slope is very gradual. To the
north, as you approach a ridge of hills, the valley floor makes a steeper
ascent. A thin mist pervades the air, offspring of the lively waters which
splash along a rocky channel.
name: A Misty Valley

desc:
A thin mist fills the air all around you, leaving dewy drops on everything it
touches. It is plain to see that this is the result of the lively little river
which bubbles through the valley. The vale itself runs from east to west, losing
altitude as it travels away from the foothills of the Misty Mountains.
name: A Misty Valley

desc:
A small river rushes through the valley, tumbling over rocks and sending up a
fine mist which hangs in the air all about you. The land rises sharply to the
north, where it climbs toward a tall ridge of hills. To the south, beyond the
river, a second ridge, foothills to the Misty Mountains, marks the southern
boundary of the vale.
name: A Misty Vale

desc:
Westward the valley loses altitude and becomes narrower as it is forced between
two long, hilly ridges. To the east it opens out as it travels through a wide
area of open plains, hemmed in only to the far south by the foothills of the
Misty Mountains. A bright brook rushes through the vale, its chilly waters
sending up a light mist all around.
name: A Misty Valley

desc:
The ground here is fairly rocky, but rich enough to support a thin blanket of
grass. To the south a small river flows by, its silty waters splashing against a
rocky bank. Sloping gently along the length of the valley, and down towards the
river, the land climbs more steeply to the north where it draws near to a ridge
of hills.
name: In a Rugged Valley

desc:
The valley runs from east to west, making a steady descent. Here the river bank
is rather rocky, but does not stand at any great height above the flowing
waters. This has apparently resulted in repeated flooding of the area, leaving
behind a rich, silty soil which supports a lush growth of dark green grass,
weeds, wildflowers and numerous small shrubs and bushes.
name: Mixed Ground

desc:
A wide but shallow river flows by to the south, its lazy waters swirling idly
by. It is hard to tell where the river really ends and the land actually begins.
A broad ribbon of shallows and mud flats stretches along the shore, a result of
the perpetual flooding and erosion of the soft, earthy bank by the unforgiving
water.
name: The Flood Plain

desc:
Though some distance from the river this place has been chosen as the site of a
camp. Further south the land becomes quite marshy, but here the ground makes a
gradual ascent, climbing toward an area of bleak, open moorland. This slope
affords at least some measure of shelter from the stiff winds which howl across
the flatlands. A small ring of stones has been set out, and next to these a
single, far larger boulder rests.
name: At an Abandoned Campsite

desc:
The ground changes slightly here, as it makes a very gentle descent toward the
river valley below. Whilst to the north there is a patchwork of coarse grass and
tufts of heather, here some hardy little bushes have sprouted; the grass is
softer and the heather is present only in isolated clumps.
name: On a Gentle Slope

desc:
Grass, rocks and heather fight for possession of this sloping ground. To the
northwest, where it draws toward a ridge of hills, the terrain becomes ever more
rocky. To the east, along the valley, it is the grass that wins out, fed by the
waters of the river below. To the northeast a vast area of open moorland
stretches before you, blanketed by coarse grass and heather.
name: Rocky Fields

desc:
A tall ridge of hills rears above you to the north, its sheer sides quite
unclimbable at this point. The land here is peppered by loose rock and stone,
which probably tumbled down from the rocky slopes above. Many lie hidden under a
thick covering of dark green grass which grows tall and strong in the shelter of
the hillside. A few small, stout bushes fill out the landscape.
name: In the Shadow of a Ridge of Hills

desc:
The valley floor slopes steeply to the north, where it nestles against the flank
of a tall ridge of hills. Eastward the land also rises, though more gently, as
it climbs steadily toward the distant Misty Mountains. The ground itself is a
mixture of lush grass and rockier patches as the two terrains mingle here.
name: On the Slopes of a Ridge of Hills

desc:
Thick grass covers the lands immediately to your south, but looking north the
land becomes sloped and rather less lush. Scattered rocks lie all about you,
fallen from the rocky ridge which looms over the landscape to the north. East
and west the valley cuts across the land, with a small river tracing its way
along the valley floor.
name: Climbing towards the Hills

desc:
More grey than green, a tortuous tangle of twisted trees rises out of the dank
haze. Moss blankets almost everything here and only a few stubborn, stunted
shrubs and scattered patches of tough, yellow grass have taken hold amongst the
moss and rocks. Some strange animal cries can be heard from somewhere deep amid
the vegetation, but it is not possible to identify the source.
name: A Musty Forest

desc:
A knotty tangle of tall, twisted trees grows up out of the seemingly perpetual
grey haze which blankets the area. A damp, musty smell permeates the air and the
undergrowth of this strangely warped woodland consists of nothing but a few
sickly clumps of weeds. To the east and north the trees thin out, giving way to
a ribbon of rough brushwood, but south and west the vegetation becomes thicker.
name: A Musty Forest

desc:
A small river travels the length of this valley. To the east the glen opens out,
but here it is funnelled between two ranges of hills. As the waters descend
toward the plains below they rush through the narrow gully, splashing over the
rocks and stones which lie in the channel, kicking up a fine mist which hangs
all around you.
name: Along a Misty Vale

desc:
Eastward the valley widens but here it is quite narrow and tall hills loom above
it, both north and south. With their rocky flanks standing tall above the valley
floor the thin mist which rises from the rushing river below has no way to
escape and the cool water hangs in the air all about you.
name: A Misty Valley

desc:
Two narrow ridges of hills rise to the west, looming high above the floor of the
valley. Its sides rise steeply here, especially on this side of the river, where
they climb toward a lofty cliff. The ground is rocky and, despite being fed by
the rich waters of the stream below, support only a thin covering of grass.
name: At the Mouth of a Narrowing Gorge

desc:
The land slopes steeply from the river up toward the cliffs to the south. Thin
clumps of tough grass compete with rocks and loose scree for dominion on this
side of the waterway. No larger plants seem able to gain a foothold despite the
nutrient-rich waters which flow by just below.
name: In a Rugged Valley

desc:
East to west the valley gently descends, carrying a small river towards a
narrowing gorge. To the far side of the waterway the landscape opens out into a
vast expanse of rolling plains, but here the ground climbs steeply, rising
toward massive cliffs. The footing is rocky, with little vegetation aside from a
thin covering of tough grass and weeds.
name: A Sloping Valley

desc:
Further west, where the valley walls rise at a more gentle gradient, travel
south would necessitate a strenuous uphill walk. Here the walls are steeper, so
that progress to the south would require at least some skill in climbing. The
ground underfoot is quite rocky, but is also level enough to retain a decent
covering of topsoil. Thus, nestled below the small cliff face, this ribbon of
land supports a rich variety of plant life, springing up between rocks and
stones, everywhere you look.
name: Along the Floor of the Valley

desc:
East and north the hillside falls off in a sheer, unclimbable cliff. To the
south the hills rear high above this smaller summit, ascending sharply into the
Misty Mountains. Westward lies the only route off this small summit, where it is
possible to scramble down a steady slope, seeking a path among the massive rocks
and boulders that are scattered across the ground.
name: A Rocky Summit

desc:
To the south the foothills of the Misty Mountains rise sharply above you, too
sheer to scale. East and west the land slopes gently, following the countours of
the valley floor below. Progress north would entail scrambling and climbing down
a steep slope covered in scree. The land here is rocky and the topsoil thin, but
under the shelter of the cliffs a number of small shrubs and weeds manage to
thrive even so.
name: Rising Valley

desc:
The ground falls off steadily to the north and west and its slope makes for only
the thinnest of topsoil here, yet a number of small bushes and coarse weeds have
managed to grow and even thrive, perhaps because a sheer cliff to the south
affords the area a good degree of protection from prevailing winds.
name: Climbing toward the Hills

desc:
Standing at some height above the plains to the north the valley wall climbs
steadily eastwards. Sheer cliffs loom to the south, blocking all travel, but the
protection they offer from high winds has meant that a few thin bushes and
coarse weeds have managed to take hold even in the thin soil of this sloping
ground.
name: A Climbing Valley

desc:
Sheer cliffs rise above you to the west and to the south. To the east the land
slopes gently upwards, climbing toward the far distant Misty Mountains. North
lies a rocky slope which leads down toward the floor of a narrow valley, where a
bright little river cuts its way across the countryside.
name: A Steep-walled Valley

desc:
Though relatively large in area the cavern has a low roof, which is fortunate
for the only apparent exit from this place is through the rough, round opening
above your head. The walls and floor are uneven and a number of bones lie
scattered all around. The most notable feature of this chamber, however, is the
smell of damp fur, mingled with that of rotting meat.
name: A Wide Cave

desc:
A few extremely narrow fissures cleave the ground, stretching off to the north
between the massive boulders. The rest of the landscape is strewn with the
colossal stones and almost no vegetation is to be seen here. The few hardy weeds
which have tried to take hold in the thin soil have been beaten down by the
smaller rocks and pebbles which cover the land between the boulders.
name: Among the Boulders

desc:
The trees grow to about three yards height, their crowns becoming rather dense
and creating a permanent dusk here. In the shadows of the trees several smaller
birds and wild animals have their dens, well out of sight from any larger bird
that might prey on them.
name: A Small Stand of Trees

desc:
A wide and dusty road runs through a cut that runs from the south and opens to
the east. The road is surfaced with stones and bricks, allowing the passage of
heavy wagons. Small insects can be found crawling underneath the stones, serving
as a meal for the many birds populating this area. Small patches of dried-out
grass grow at the sides of the road.
name: Bend in the Ancient Broken Road

desc:
Small stones dot the bleakness of the dusty path as it cuts its way east and
west through the valley. Small pieces of brush grow sporadically on the barren
red slopes giving little to no shelter for any wildlife. The northern wall of
the valley seems to be unclimbable, while to the south lie some passable hills.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
What was once a highly trafficked road is nothing more than a few odd pieces of
flat stones heaved upward by the forces of nature on this trail. Long grass
grows between the stones. The ground levels out into a plateau that extends far
to the south. In the north a ridge rises up above the plains, making it quite
impossible to travel in that direction. Hardly any traces of animal life can be
seen here, save for the occasional bird, high aloft in the distance.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The broken stones and barren, dusty tracks that identify the course of the road
point down to the north as well as to the west, following the contour of the
valley. To the east are yet more plains, while to the south lie more of the
semi-barren plains that make up this region.
name: Bend in the Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The faint outline of the trail bends to the south and east here, bordered by a
swift roaring river to the north. The trail is composed of overgrown flat stones
patched into a road. A tall ridge rises up to the west hindering all movement in
that direction, and to the south, the plains continue almost unbroken for some
miles.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The sound of rushing water can be heard to the north, where a wooden bridge
spans a lively river, to the west the well-trodden path of the Ancient Broken
Road continues. In the east, the river continues its meandering flow, while a
trail continues south towards what appears to be a camp.
name: Bend in the Ancient Broken Road

desc:
A sturdy wooden bridge has been erected here, spanning the waters that rush
swiftly by beneath. A cover has been added overhead, though seemingly as an
afterthought, as the construction and workmanship does not seem to match the
rest of the structure. Looking eastward over the wooden railing, one can see the
waters of two streams pick up momentum as they collide, forming a swift river
that continues far into the west.
name: Covered Bridge over a Confluence

desc:
The soil of the path is springy and moist, being close to the crossing of the
river to the southeast. On the east, the river flows past inaccessible banks,
while to the south, a wooden bridge spans the width of the river as it passes
underneath and continues west.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The path of the road turns here to the south and east. Searching to the distant
south, a long wooden bridge is visible. This is the low point of the valley with
mostly plains and water marking its features. To the west however, a patch of
densely packed trees rises up into the sky.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The faint outline of the road bends to the west and north here, bordered on the
south by the steadily flowing waters of a wide stream. To the east, an expanse
of grassland opens out, initially soggy from the constant supply of water, but
becoming drier further away. A shallow dike has been dug out to protect the road
from the wetlands.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The road follows a north and south path, intersecting the downward slope of this
valley. The occasional tracks of wildlife can be seen faintly in the dirt beside
the road, leading eastward towards a small river that can barely be seen in the
distance. To the west lie rocky fields which perhaps have the saving grace of
changing the monotony of the landscape.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The worn path of the Ancient Broken Road makes a sharp bend here, although a
small trail can be seen branching off to the west. A languid river runs its
course through the landscape in the north, curving along the side of a wide
plateau. To the east and south, the Ancient Broken Road continues for many
miles.
name: Bend in the Ancient Broken Road

desc:
Bordered by a shallow river and its gently tumbling waters, the Ancient Broken
Road continues its east-west direction. The road is mainly composed of large
flat stones, although time has taken its toll and grass is quite a dominant
feature in between the plates. Some leagues away to the east bumpy foothills
give way for the towering Misty Mountains beyond. To the north, the river bank
is impassable, while to the south dense, thick brush also blocks passage.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The road runs to the west and north here, blocked to the south by a natural
barricade of rock and stone. The barricade itself is not very tall, but the drop
on the far side would be a formidable challenge for even the most highly skilled
climber. The land slopes off into a valley to the south and east, eventually
meeting with a muddy waterway. To the northeast, another river can be seen
softly flowing from the foothills of the Misty Mountains to the lowlands in the
west.
name: Bend in the Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The land is sparsely covered with bushes and shrubs, but mostly comprised of a
dry grass. It slopes gradually to the south and east, where a muddy stream can
be seen. Another body of water can be seen to the north, cutting along the base
of a steep plateau. To the south, the riverbank cuts harshly into the plains,
preventing any travel in that direction.
name: Sloping Grasslands

desc:
Small patches of bare earth mix in with the occasional stone in these otherwise
sparsely vegetated grasslands. A pile of mud covered branches seems to cover a
hole in the ground. Piles of mashed skulls litter the ground, suggesting that
some violent predators might live behind the branches. To the south lay a swamp,
while the plains continue east and west.
name: Sparse Grasslands

desc:
To the east the Misty Mountains tower up over this barren grassland. Sheer,
rocky slopes lead up to the jagged, snow-clad peaks, gnawing on the horizon like
sharp teeth. A narrow ridge lies to the north, while the sound of water can be
heard from the muddy stream in the south.
name: Sparse Grasslands

desc:
Aside from the sporadic patches of sod underfoot, not much seems to be living in
these foothills of the Misty Mountains. The dirt is packed down here and is
coloured dark brown with streaks of red. Some bushes dot the windy landscape.
name: Scattered Grasses

desc:
Flowing from east to west, the river cuts a wide path across the roadway here.
At some time in the past great effort was made to make this ford an easy one to
pass, by laying large, flat boulders in the bed to make the already shallow
waters yet shallower. But through years of disrepair many have been jarred quite
loose, and today the traveller should take care in making the crossing.
name: Fording a Tributary

desc:
Drawing close to a river the land becomes more fertile. A gentle incline climbs
from the ford toward a flat expanse of higher ground to the north. To the south
there are signs of periodic flooding by the river, bringing rich deposits of
red-brown silt to nourish the grass and brush. The resulting jumble of grassy
tussocks, weeds and brush thrives at the expense of the pavement, and so the
road is almost completely lost to nature.
name: Road near a Ford

desc:
A rough patchwork of broken pavers and tussocks of thin yellowing grass define
the ancient roadway. Strangely, though this is such an exposed location, here it
is that the road is in perhaps the best state of repair for many leagues. Here
also the tufts of grass seem to be smaller and fewer, and perhaps herein lies
the explanation for the condition of the pavement; the fierce, chill winds from
the northern reaches of the Misty Mountains, and the thin layer of topsoil have
less effect on the cold, hard stones than on the encroaching vegetation.
name: Road across the Plateau

desc:
Winding from south to east, the road meanders down a gentle slope. Little more
here than a rough trail through the coarse grass, the road has certainly given
in to the onslaught of wind and weather that has, through long years, conspired
with the action of root and foot to assault its once well-kept surface. Just
north of here, a small building nestles close by the rising plain.
name: Turning Road

desc:
Turning here from its north to south course, the old road begins to make a
gradual, slightly winding ascent toward a flat table of land above the plain,
passing by a battered stone structure as it does so. The pavement here is, if
anything, in an even worse state of repair than it is on the flat lands to the
north. The broken remains of the pavers have succumbed to gravity and many of
the pieces have tumbled down the gentle grade, to accumulate in a broad and
tumbled pile here at the foot of the slope.
name: Road at the Foot of a Slope

desc:
Nestled by the foot of a rising escarpment, a stone building of modest size
stands beside the roadway here. A small lean-to, perhaps to shelter fire wood,
stands empty against the wall of the building. The windows are broken, and the
roof shows signs of several developing holes. Yet the hard-packed earth in front
of the doorway is virtually devoid of vegetation, suggesting that somebody's
feet still tramp along the broken roadway and into the structure.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
Due north and south, the road stretches across a bleak and windy plain, running
almost parallel to the far distant Misty Mountains. Massive paving stones lie
shattered and broken among the coarse tufts of grass, but their ruined remains
still show clearly that this was once a broad roadway, created to allow several
people to travel abreast.
name: Ruined Road across the Plain

desc:
Tall, moss-covered trees loom above the road to the east, but westward it is
flanked only by rotting and burned stumps, that nestle amid a sea of grass and
rank weeds. The road itself is badly broken, its mighty pavers shattered over
the years by the action of frost and vegetation.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The road stretches south into the distance, as it journeys past a dark, twisted
forest and onward across a blustery plain. The pavement from the road is so
badly shattered that it has become quite obscured amongst the thick tufts of
coarse grass. Beyond the road to the north a slight incline within the earth
begins.
name: Road by the Forest

desc:
So few and scattered, and so broken are the great stones that once comprised
this roadway, that its path is almost lost here. Looking up the slope to the
north, the trail becomes plainer, but its route from this point southwards
through the land can only be guessed at. A dark and twisted forest stands due
south, but to the southwest the way seems clearer.
name: Curve on the Ancient Broken Road

desc:
The road continues onward to the south and north, bordered to the east by a
tall, yet strangely twisted woodland. The road steadily descends to the south,
as the land falls away toward a vast expanse of windswept plains. At one time
this thoroughfare must have been an impressive sight. Even today it can be seen
that the pavement could once accommodate several travellers riding abreast, but
now most of the paving lies broken, and much of it is scattered, leaving only a
shadow of how great this road must once have been.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
As it journeys past the woods that stand southeast of here, the road makes a
gentle ascent toward the high point of a small, windswept plateau. It then turns
south and begins its descent into the broad flatland below. The paving under
foot has been invaded through the years by thick patches of yellowing grass, and
today the stones lie scattered amidst the vegetation, so that the roadway is
almost lost entirely.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
Drawing near to a dark forest, the road turns to the east, skirting beside the
woodlands. The stonework that was once so carefully laid has been broken apart
over long years of neglect by thick tufts of yellowing grass, and now for the
most part appears more like stony grassland than a well-wrought thoroughfare.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
This broken stonework was once laid with great skill and care to ease the
otherwise arduous journey through these wild lands. The pieces lie scattered and
spread, a mere remnant of this road's rumoured greatness. Sturdy grasses have
crept closer, slowly recovering the few yards of land that mankind borrowed over
the years. The abandoned remains of the road continue both north and south from
here, stretching towards the distant horizon in a thin straight line.
name: Abandoned Broken Road

desc:
Below your feet lie the broken remains of a once carefully laid road. Its broad
stonework covers several yards in width, allowing a large number of horsemen to
ride in formation. Now the stone-work is cracked and broken, the old road
abandoned and the land slowly being reclaimed by the forces of the vast grassy
plains that surround it.
name: Abandoned Broken Road

desc:
Running straight in a line north to south the old abandoned road lies bordered
by vast and wild plains, stretching eternally towards the horizon in every
direction. The road itself sits almost hidden beneath all the dirt and sturdy
grasses that cling to the stones. A few larger stone markers stand to the side
as a reminder of the great importance this now ruined road once held, as the
main path to Khazad-dum.
name: Abandoned Broken Road

desc:
This desolate crag marks the southwestern extreme of the plateau, and also its
highest point. Scoured by the fierce winds that howl across this bleak part of
the country, the land here has only the thinnest covering of soil over the
underlying rock. A few hardy patches of coarse grass and scattered weeds cling
to life, their roots struggling to find purchase in any small nook or cranny.
Rolling gently downward to the east, the ground gradually becomes less exposed,
the plants it supports healthier and less sparse.
name: Edge of a Plateau

desc:
A fairly low cliff overlooks the plain here, and the land gently climbs to the
east, drawing ever closer to the height of the plateau above. Although the rock
face is not tall, it is quite smooth, and the ground undercuts it just a little
at its base, offering an environment in which a thick tangle of prickly briers
can flourish, sheltered from the cold northern winds, but not completely from
the life-sustaining rains. A soft blanket of heather covers the slope.
name: Gentle Incline

desc:
A faint putrid smell pervades the air that seems almost to stagnate among the
mossy boles standing all about. The trees appear to be quite old, judging by
their height and girth, but they have taken on a sickly hue in this dank air.
Whatever pervades this woodland also besets the undergrowth, which is comprised
of scattered patches of yellowing grass and weeds, interspersed with only a few
spindly bushes.
name: Dank Stretch of Woods

desc:
The wide valley floor climbs northward, approaching a lonely ridge of hills. The
ground is fertile, fed by the lively stream below you, and thick, lush grass
covers the sloping terrain. The closer you come to hills, the more rocky the
land becomes.
name: Sloping Vale

desc:
The large, flat shards of shattered paving stones and countless smaller rocky
fragments lie scattered amid the grass and shrubs. A small glade of pines begins
here, where it circles along the northern border here and curves in the distant
south-west, protecting the end of the valley here. The valley expands to the
east from here. Medium and large boulders are scattered throughout the northern
area where the pines begin.
name: Glade near the Valley

desc:
Looming out of the plains, a rocky ridge of hills rears up to the east, making
the ground slope above quite precipitous. By contrast, the ground slopes down
gently to the south as a narrow valley opens out into the plains. A number of
scrubby bushes have sprouted from the rather rocky terrain, growing greater in
number, yet somehow more sickly as they approach the forest to the north.
name: Foot of a Ridge of Hills

desc:
A few stunted bushes and some scattered patches of sickly, yellow grass begin to
overtake the slippery moss that carpets the ground further north. But the trees
here are still assaulted by the creeping moss. More grey than green, they grow
upward in strange, knotty tangles, as if seeking refuge in the heights from the
encroaching moss.
name: Musty Forest

desc:
Green-black moss creeps up the trunk of every tree, almost smothering them. The
branches strive to escape this blanketing presence, twisting and writhing into
seemingly unnatural shapes. Instead of the usual undergrowth associated with
woodlands, the ground here is carpeted by the same dank moss that assails the
trees, making for a slippery footing indeed.
name: Forest of Twisted and Gloomy Trees

desc:
The walls here are covered in a dark, slimey layer of what appears to be a
putrid amalgamation of rotting insects, small animal corpses, and decaying plant
matter. Pinpricks of light can be seen through the thick tangle of roots and
plant matter above, suggesting that egress is possible but not without pointed
effort. Downward, root and decay weave together in a sickly blanket, with
nothing but darkness discernible beyond.
name: Bottom of the Forest

desc:
Sickly, stunted shrubs have sprouted from the somewhat rocky ground. To the
north they grow greater in density, and yet begin to assume an ever more sickly
pallor. The fallen leaves that drift this way from the tall trees should have
helped create a rich, fertile and loamy soil, but instead they have carried with
them the creeping moss that befouls the woodland to the north.
name: Eaves of a Twisted Forest

desc:
From somewhere deeper in the woodland, a strange animal cry can be heard, but it
is not possible to pinpoint its source. The air here seems strangely still, and
a foetid aroma pervades everything. A fuzzy coating of green-black moss covers
almost everything here, choking out all but the most persistent under- growth.
name: Musty Forest near a Road

desc:
Whereas further north a field of charred stumps attests to the fact that once a
forest thrived in this area, the land here is devoid of even that clue to the
past. The ground shows scars not only of fire, but also of the action of mortal
hands. Gaping holes in the scorched earth are all that remains to mark the
places where once large trees must have grown. No shrub, weed, nor vegetation of
any kind has yet sprung up in the blackened ground, as if somehow the earth
itself has been poisoned. A small building stands immediately to the south.
name: Charred Ground

desc:
At some time in the past, a forest covered this land. Now, however, all that
remains of the trees are blackened stumps. Yet it is apparent that this was no
accidental fire. Nor was it fire that razed the forest, for the shape of the
stumps speaks to the work of an axe in the felling of the once mighty trees. It
would seem that someone purposefully destroyed the trees, and feeling that was
not enough, set it alight, destroying it even to the smallest shrub or sapling.
name: Plains with Blackened Stumps

desc:
Thick tussocks of coarse grass compete with dense clumps of dark purple heather
to create a colourful patchwork that blankets the ground. A sombre dark brown
border of scorched vegetation bounds this area to the east, where a sizeable
expanse of land has been scarred by fire. A few miles to the southeast, a small
stone structure stands close to the foot of an escarpment, but there is no other
hint at habitation in the surrounding countryside.
name: Patchwork Plain

desc:
Small, red-brown rocks and a few larger boulders lie tumbled amid the coarse,
yellow grasses and occasional patches of thick, purple heather that clothe this
bleak and windswept plain. Not far to the south, a tall plateau looms above the
lowland, but in all other directions the land for many miles around stretches
out flat, and is seemingly featureless.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
Scattered rocks freckle the landscape, though most lie almost hidden beneath a
tufted beard of purple heather, and yellow-green grass. The land marches for
several furlongs in every direction with scarcely a feature that stands above
the flat, but rugged surface, save for an occasional dogged patch of briars that
seems to cling tenaciously to life in this bleak and windswept environment. Yet,
in the distance to the south, drawing close to a sombre plateau, both rock and
bramble become more numerous.
name: Featureless Flatlands

desc:
Far to the east the Misty Mountains form a barrier that can be traversed at only
a few passes. Closer at hand, a tall plateau stands to the southeast, but that
is yet some miles distant. In the immediate surroundings, there is a gently
rolling plain, clad mostly in heather and coarse grass, with only an occasional
small patch of brush.
name: Rugged Plains

desc:
By a strange trick of perspective, the silhouette of the plateau that looms
above the plains at some distance to the south looks like the prow of a mighty
sailing ship, cutting through the expansive green sea of grasses that spread in
all other directions, rippling like water against the wind.
name: Plains

desc:
Low tussocks of coarse, yellow-green grass and patches of dark purple heather
clothe the landscape. A few isolated briars send spindly limbs snaking out amid
the ground cover, their sharp thorns threatening to ensnare the unwary, but no
taller vegetation is to be seen for many leagues in any direction.
name: Coarse Grassland

desc:
The land folds slightly here, creating a narrow gully. Though quite shallow at
this point, the cleft becomes both deeper and wider as it stretches out to the
east. A few small, thorny bushes crowd in the sparse shelter offered by the low
walls of the channel, and as those walls grow taller, the briars grow larger and
more numerous as well.
name: Narrow Cleft

desc:
Coarse grasses and low purple heather compete for dominance on a broad expanse
of lazily undulating plains. This windswept landscape stretches far into the
distance in all directions; in the immediate vicinity, nothing taller than a
short clump of grass stands above ground level, and even that bends, straining
to stand upright against the prevailing winds.
name: Gently Rolling Plain

desc:
Though the land here is rugged and uncultivated, not far to the north is an area
that bears the scars of civilisation. East to west, atop a low dike, an old and
rotting fence marks the boundary of a small-holding that has apparently long
lain abandoned to the ravages of nature, and the predations of whatever beasts
roam the countryside hereabouts.
name: Tumbled Ground

desc:
The bleak and windswept plains begin to descend to the north suddenly, where a
rocky incline defines the transition that leads to a shallow trench. At the
margin of the gully, the thin limbs of a low patch of brambles snake out amid
the heather and creep around the tussocks of coarse, yellow-green grass.
name: Wasteland near a Trench

desc:
A deep trench opens out to the north, a ragged wound that dissects this flat,
and otherwise largely featureless tract of land. For miles in every direction
this fissure represents the only break in the monotony of a landscape marked by
nothing more distinguishing than the few small brambles that grow amid the
endless sea of heather and bracken.
name: Harsh Terrain

desc:
The land dips and rolls in a series of heather-clad folds that stretch away
north and west, growing both wider and deeper as they draw near to a yawning
trench. Low, rugged tussocks of grass peep up along the lines defined by the
little gullies, cutting a yellowing scar across the ground, and bending in the
blustery winds that seem to perpetually swirl across this bleak expanse.
name: Exposed Land

desc:
A field of stumps that stands to the east gradually gives way to an expanse of
grass and heather-clad land, with a scattering of low, thorny shrubs. Isolated
tussocks of coarse grass, and a number of small, shallow clefts in the rolling
landscape conspire in their attempt to trip the unwary traveller.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
This land was once a part of a thick forest, the last vestige of which looms at
some distance to the east. But every tree here was felled long ago, leaving only
a field of hefty stumps. The demise of the trees has made way for the growth of
a profusion of small, sturdy brambles and a confused carpet of tangled weeds
that blankets the ground.
name: Bramble-tangled Stumps

desc:
Looking east, across the road, a dank and musty forest rises out of the land.
Here, however, where once the woodland continued, there now remains only fields
of dark and scorched stumps. The mighty trees met with the bite of cold iron,
felled by axe; many of the massive stumps that remained have been licked by
fire. Further to the south, drawing close to a small stone structure, the
destruction becomes more marked, as if protecting that building from the
denizens of the forest were the motivation behind the assault.
name: Field of Scorched Stumps

desc:
Patches of dark bracken compete with heather, grass and moss to blanket the
gently undulating land. A few rangy brambles stretch their thorny limbs across
the ground, grasping out at passing feet with their sharp barbs. To both west
and north the otherwise gently rolling landscape plunges rather more abruptly,
opening out into quite a deep and decidedly steep-walled gully.
name: Above a Turning Trench

desc:
Small, scattered patches of briars stand amid the coarse grass and heather that
dominate the landscape. This thin shroud of vegetation is not enough to mask the
small fissures and rolling undulations in the ground. Running from east to west,
these folds grow much larger to the north. In the distance, one of them opens up
to form a much deeper chasm.
name: Rugged Heath

desc:
While much of the surrounding land supports no plant life except thick tussocks
of coarse grasses, here a mass of thorny bushes has sprung up, extending from
the denser tangle of furze that thrives in the shallow gully to the north. The
briars reach off into the distance to the east and south, where they fight for
dominance with coarse weeds that flourish in a field of stumps. A ridge running
along the western side prevents passage in that direction.
name: Bushy Plain

desc:
Opening out to the west, the gully spans little over a yard here at its eastern
end. The northern boundary of the gully gains height as it continues to rise to
the west, blocking any passage to the north from here. To the south is a gentle
grade which leads out of the shallow trench. Even though the channel is very
shallow, it has nevertheless served to accumulate a thicker and richer covering
of soil and to collect the waters running off the lands to either side. As a
result a thick tangle of brush has sprouted up in this area. The trench curves
along the eastern side, lined along the top with thick shrubs and bushes, also
impassable from this area of the trench.
name: End of a Trench

desc:
Massive stumps stand all around, the last tokens of what was once an area of
thick woodland. Where once the trees were dominant, now a bristling field of
thorny briars has taken control of the landscape. Coarse weeds weave a snarled
mess about the stems of the brambles, many climbing and curling over the ruined
tree stumps. A tall ridge stands behind the vegetation here.
name: Ruined Remains of a Thick Forest

desc:
A tall plateau looms above the plains just east of here. Its sheer wall offers
few visible hand or footholds, and appears quite impossible to climb. Although
the plateau affords the land here some degree of protection from the prevailing
winds, it casts a deep shadow for much of the day, depriving the vegetation of
light. All that survives here grows weak and tall, as if striving to find its
way to the sun.
name: Foot of a Plateau

desc:
The land rolls down from the north in mighty waves. But here they seem to halt
abruptly, due to a large ridge that forms along the north-west that curves along
the southern area of land here, and then it travels to the north rising higher
as it goes. Rocks and boulders take up a lot of the space between the coarse
grasses, shrubs and briars scattered over the land.
name: Fold in the Land

desc:
Whilst the terrain hereabouts is characterised by coarse grasses, growing in
scattered clumps, here the landscape has a softer countenance. Purple heather
fights for dominance with a blanket of lush, dark green grass. Such a contrast
between this area and much of the surrounding lands suggests that at one time
this land was farmed. Yet there is no trace of recent use, hinting that the
acreage has long since been abandoned.
name: Abandoned Pasture

desc:
A deep, steep-sided gully dominates the land to the south, like a massive scar
that stretches east and west across the plains. East and west a vast expanse of
thick, dark green grass and purple heather lies, offering no promise of shelter
from the prevailing winds that sweep across the countryside. In the distant
north, the last vestiges of some ancient habitation stand amongst the ruins.
name: Brink of a Steep-walled Trench

desc:
Bordered to the north by a low earth bank and to the south by a wide and rather
deep gully, the land here is an ocean of dark purple heather, mingled with dark
green grass. These lands are so much lusher than the surrounding countryside
that they must once have been pastures for the abandoned farming community that
lies to the northwest. Further west the vegetation becomes considerably more
coarse, as if there the local flora had more quickly regained complete
domination.
name: Ruined Acreage

desc:
Stretching east and west, a low bank rises above the current elevation. In the
distance beyond, a few crumbled ruins can be seen. It is apparent that this land
was once part of a farm or smallholding as the ruins of an old, stone wall stand
at the brink of a deep gully that lies to the south. It is equally clear that it
has been many long years since anyone tended this acreage, as the lush grasses
that must once have blanketed the entire areas are now fighting for dominance
with the dark purple heather that is so common hereabouts.
name: Forsaken Fields

desc:
Just a short march to the north, a low, earthen embankment stretches from east
to west across the land. Though the bank is not especially high, a sturdy pile
of timber and stone has been heaped along its length, creating a crude, but
nevertheless quite impenetrable barrier. Yet this would seem to have been an odd
act, for the lands nearby were apparently once part of the now ruined
smallholding that lies to the north.
name: Nearing a Ruined Structure

desc:
Amid a nearly impenetrable labyrinth of briars, it is difficult to see the walls
of the trench. From an occasional thin spot in the prickly vegetation it is
possible to see that the gully makes a noticeable turn here, travelling to the
south and to the east. Its walls are quite tall, and only by climbing can the
plains beyond be reached.
name: Turning Trench

desc:
So steep and tall are the walls of the trench that the entire area is enveloped
in shadow for much of the day. Yet despite this a thick snarl of briars thrives
in the gully, their sharp and grasping barbs making travel along the fissure
difficult at best. Loose rocks and scree characterise the walls both north and
south, making escape from the entangling shrubs by that route impossible.
name: Shadowed Gully

desc:
Although the trench is quite wide here, its walls are very steep, and comprised
primarily of loose rocks and sharp scree, rendering ascent impossible. Deep
shadows engulf this area for much of the day, but a dense tangle of prickly
shrubs has come to dominate the area nevertheless; their thorny branches impede
progress to the west, where the vegetation grows more prolific. To the east the
gully becomes noticeably shallower, and the plant life slightly less dense.
name: Deep Rift

desc:
Turning from its north-south route to travel westward, the trench becomes
noticeably shallower. Here, at its turning point, the gully remains deep, and
its sides so sheer that only by climbing these walls could the plain above be
reached. Journeying along the fissure is little easier, as a dense tangle of
thorny bushes fills the ground from wall to wall, their sharp spines tearing at
any creature that brushes by.
name: Deep along a Fissure

desc:
The ground slopes upward to both the north and south. The slopes define the
walls of a fairly shallow gully that rises higher to the east and west across
the plains. Sloping at a gentler grade, the land climbs out of the cleft to the
west, but to the east the fissure grows deeper with sharp brambles that almost
fills the cleft, becoming ever more tightly packed and making progress that way
rather difficult.
name: Shallow Gully

desc:
The land rolls gently down toward a fairly shallow gully that runs west and
north-east across the countryside. Heather and coarse grass covers the ground
all about, but the long, spindly arms of a dense tangle of briers grope from the
margins of the trench, their sharp thorns seeming to strive for any flesh that
they might be able to tear into.
name: Shallow Turning Trench

desc:
The floor of this little grotto is covered in a thin coating of soft, red-brown
dust and its walls are quite dry. Apparently someone has made their home here,
for a heavy animal skin has been set across the mouth of the cave to shelter it
from the winds outside. A thick bed of dried grass lies by the rear wall, while
a small ring of stones sits close to the entrance. A number of small fissures
fairly high in the front wall expose the interior to any natural light outside.
name: Dry Cave

desc:
Dark, dry, and an extension from the cavern to the west this small cave looks as
if it was meant to be larger. The walls all appear to be half dug out along the
base to the middle section of the cavern and then continue toward the top but in
a very narrow manner. An echo from the continuous scuffling from above can be
heard from this portion of the cave.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Set on a gently rising plain, this must once have been a well-tended garden. It
has now fallen into decay, its terraced beds overgrown with brush, weeds and
tough grasses. A wooden fence stands around the plot, its planks loose and
rotting, and a dead tree stands toward the middle of the garden. Thorny shrubs
with vines and ivy that twist between branches now grow along the southern
portion of the garden, restricting movement any further in that direction. To
the east stands an averaged size shed, worn from the weather and time, attached
to a much larger building behind it.
name: Rotten Garden

desc:
The flat plains start to give way here to a rising bluff that climbs toward a
rocky plateau. The slope climbs steadily southward, the ground covered by a
shaggy stubble of grassy tussocks. To the east an old wooden fence marks the
boundary between the wild lands and a small oasis of what might once have been
termed civilisation.
name: Foot of an Escarpment

desc:
A low cliff rises out of the plain, a red-brown wall of sandstone pocked with
numerous cracks and fissures. As it sweeps northeast the rock face loses height
and begins to merge into an area characterised by a gentler grade. Thorny shrubs
grow along the foot of the cliff, and a proliferation of grasses and weeds form
a patchwork carpet underfoot.
name: Foot of a Cliff

desc:
Rising quite precipitously out of the plains, an immense plateau presents an
